identity.title: Require production-runtime truth for product and phase completion

authorization.reference: Operator requested rule changes preventing mockups, vibe coding, scaffolding, and test-satisfying substitutes from shipping or advancing phases without working runtime behavior, then explicitly authorized implementation.

lifecycle.next_action: Await Operator selection before activating any later work-packet stub.

scope.summary: Make operator-usable production runtime behavior the mandatory product-progress and phase-closure unit, and mechanically prevent mocks, scaffolds, prototypes, tests, or self-authored reports from substituting for the shipped artifact.
in_scope: Revise and extend Codex authority with enforceable runtime-truth rules
Add matching build rules and work-packet requirements
Replace skeleton-only product phases with runtime vertical slices without narrowing final product scope
Implement a fail-closed runtime-truth gate and integrate it into canonical verification
Add negative fixtures and unit tests for test-only, mock, scaffold, no-op, missing-artifact, and missing-counterfactual bypasses
Update the rule-to-proof map, ID registry, taskboard, and no-context model manual
Run governance, architecture, Rust, negative-fixture, and canonical PR validation
non_goals: Implementing Ferric extraction, download, archive, watcher, or FFmpeg product behavior
Claiming any Ferric runtime capability is currently implemented
Forbidding mocks or deterministic fixtures as supporting unit-test tools
Deleting final product scope or the operator-facing plugin starter-generation capability
Changing the authorized Rust plus FFmpeg and ffprobe dependency boundary
Modifying WP-FF-004 remediation source or its uncommitted work
dependencies: Existing Rust xtask and locked dependency set
Current three-root repository boundary and canonical verification gates
Cargo production profiles and process execution
No new runtime, native, test, or validator dependency

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Codex, build, and work-packet authority make operator-usable behavior from the shipped production entrypoint the only legal product-progress and phase-completion proof; tests, mocks, fixtures, reports, prototypes, and scaffolding are supporting evidence only.
evidence: FF-CODEX-049 through FF-CODEX-059
FF-BUILD-078 through FF-BUILD-088
FF-WPR-041 through FF-WPR-049
FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001 negative fixtures

acceptance_matrix.requirement: The implementation sequence no longer contains a completed skeleton-only product phase: build bootstrap is a non-product prerequisite, the first product phase is a complete direct-HTTP/HLS cross-boundary runtime slice, and later phase wording requires runnable vertical outcomes without dropping final scope.
evidence: Technical design Section 28 now distinguishes non-product Prerequisite 0 from runtime product phases
Phase 1 is the first complete shipped-entrypoint runtime slice
Phase 9 requires generated plugin build, install, and production-host execution

acceptance_matrix.requirement: FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001 validates a strict versioned runtime-proof manifest, production artifact identity, clean staged execution contract, production boundary use, observables, negative paths, and counterfactual detection; product-affecting changes cannot convert absent runtime proof into PASS or NOT_APPLICABLE.
evidence: build/tools/fforager-xtask/src/main.rs runtime_truth_check
Exact activation-checkpoint proof binding
Locked release build and artifact digest
Repository-external clean stage and external-process execution
Success, negative, and same-oracle counterfactual validation

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Executable negative fixtures prove rejection of mock/test-only proof, scaffold or prototype completion, no-op success, missing artifact identity, missing clean-package execution, and absent counterfactual evidence.
evidence: Eight runtime-* architecture fixtures execute the production contract validator
34 Rust tests include proof predeclaration, stage aliases, unsafe paths, base rewrite, mock/test-only, no-op, and counterfactual probes

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Formatting, warnings-denied Clippy, full Rust tests, architecture-check, verify-pr, governance parsing, ID registry, authority mirror, allowed-path diff, and adversarial post-change review pass without claiming current Ferric runtime behavior.
evidence: cargo fmt --check PASS
warnings-denied cargo clippy PASS
34 Rust tests PASS
FF-GATE-ARCH-001 PASS
FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001 governance-only PASS with explicit no-product ceiling
Canonical verify-pr, governance, mirror, registry, and containment evidence is recorded before VALIDATED closure

checks_run: Read START_HERE.yaml, AGENTS.md, .GOV/codex.yaml, taskboard, active WP-FF-004 packet, topology, build rules, work-packet requirements/template, design phase anchors, readiness matrix, verifier implementation, and ID registry
Verified AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md are byte-identical
Inspected current branch, HEAD, merge-base, dirty paths, and worktrees
Created isolated locked worktree from committed WP-FF-004 activation without copying its dirty remediation work
Checked current Rust, Cargo, Google engineering, GitHub artifact provenance, SLSA, and field Rust CLI sources
Implemented runtime-truth authority and phase rewrite
Implemented strict packet applicability, activation proof binding, artifact identity, repository-external staging, external execution, observables, negative scenarios, and counterfactual validation
Ran warnings-denied Clippy and 32-test suites before the final two boundary tests were added
Ran FF-GATE-ARCH-001 with eight runtime negative fixtures
Ran FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001 and inspected policy-only proof classes and explicit no-product limitation
Performed independent adversarial review and closed base rewrite, shipping-policy downgrade, stage collision, symlink, path alias, in-repository staging, and output-containment bypasses
Ran final warnings-denied Clippy and 34 Rust tests
Ran canonical FF-GATE-PR-001 and inspected its generated report
Parsed changed JSON, checked allowed paths, registry ranges, authority mirror, and git diff whitespace
last_checkpoint.next_action: Commit the validated containment record and clear canonical active focus.

PASS: closure commit 75dbeb495694955f6a0ff786f7bcb47b73d5bbeb and MERGE_PENDING evidence commit 4b7fec613a2d5de0d1136ed847cc0021a90a8b52 are ancestors of main merge 142d0aad625713231f91381deb709e174b2865f7
PASS: post-merge formatting, warnings-denied Clippy, all 49 integrated tests, FF-GATE-ARCH-001, and FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001
not_proven: A packaged Ferric binary, because no shipped product member exists yet
Release readiness or yt-dlp replacement parity

required_outcomes: Product progress and phase closure require operator-usable behavior from the exact shipped artifact
Tests, mocks, reports, prototypes, and scaffolding remain supporting evidence only
Every product packet predeclares success, negative, and counterfactual runtime proof
Missing runtime proof fails closed instead of becoming PASS or NOT_APPLICABLE
risk_cases: A packet rewrites its base SHA or adds runtime proof after implementation to hide unproven product work
A shipping-policy change reclassifies a product member and escapes product-impact detection
A staged input overwrites the binary or an expected output aliases an input or gate receipt
A mock, test-only adapter, no-op exit zero, or fabricated observable is offered as product completion
A symlink or unsafe path escapes the clean stage or fixture ownership boundary

reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.candidate: no mechanism beyond the accepted outcome
reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.result: Rejected because current authority and verifier explicitly permit skeleton-only PASS.
Selected for the existing xtask process runner, reports, active-packet resolver, policy parser, and negative-fixture framework.
Selected for strict Serde validation already present, hashing, filesystem staging, process execution, and bounded checks.
Selected for Cargo production builds, Git source identity, filesystem isolation, and required FFmpeg/ffprobe preflight when scenarios activate them.
Existing serde, serde_json, sha2, and toml dependencies cover the gate; no addition is justified.
Selected as strict manifest validation plus canonical gate integration and mutation fixtures, without a new product crate.

artifact_specific_justification.codex_rules: Define the canonical prohibition and completion truth.
artifact_specific_justification.build_rules: Translate the authority into executable proof obligations.
artifact_specific_justification.packet_contract: Require runtime scenarios before product implementation and closure.
artifact_specific_justification.technical_design: Remove skeleton-only phase completion from the product sequence.
artifact_specific_justification.xtask: Fail closed when product changes omit or fake production runtime evidence.
artifact_specific_justification.negative_fixtures: Prove the validator rejects the named regression paths.
artifact_specific_justification.model_manual: Tell no-context models how runtime truth is established and recovered.

change_evidence.pre_simplification_verdict: PASS: the original authority and verifier were proven insufficient because skeleton phases and governance-only PASS could coexist with no shipped Ferric runtime artifact.
simplification_changes: Replaced subjective anti-vibe language with exact shipped-entrypoint, artifact-identity, observable, negative-path, and counterfactual invariants.
Kept one runtime-proof schema and one canonical runtime gate instead of adding parallel completion systems.
Generalized artifact justification validation instead of preserving a WP-FF-003-specific key list.
Merged skeleton product stages into non-product prerequisites and the first complete runtime slice without deleting final scope.
change_evidence.post_simplification_verdict: PASS: the resulting contract has one fail-closed applicability path, one strict runtime manifest, and direct negative probes for every operator-named substitute class.
complete_post_change_verification: Parse every changed JSON and canonical YAML authority surface
Verify AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md remain byte-identical
Verify ID registry ranges and the allowed-path diff
Inspect the generated runtime report for policy-only proof classes and explicit no-product limitations
Run git diff --check and prove closure commit containment in main

ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.current_ceiling: The gate can enforce proof structure and reject bypasses now, but no Ferric production artifact exists to execute.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.replacement_trigger: The first shipped product member or product-affecting packet activates mandatory packaged-artifact execution.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.likely_upgrade_path: Add versioned runtime scenarios per capability and execute the exact staged release artifact through the same manifest contract.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.trigger_detection_method: Architecture member inventory, packet product-impact declaration, changed-path classification, and runtime-proof manifest presence fail closed.

DIFF_ATTACK_SURFACES: Product-impact classification and packet-controlled base SHA
Cargo package, binary, profile, features, and shipped-member identity
Artifact copy, digest binding, repository-external stage, and input/output paths
Process environment, timeout, exit status, stdout, stderr, files, and counterfactual oracle
Phase and completion wording across Codex, build, packet, design, and model-manual surfaces
INDEPENDENT_CHECKS_RUN: Read the implementation diff independently after the first green test run
Compared changed-path classification against both current and base shipping policy
Inspected stage construction, artifact rehash, fixture canonicalization, symlink handling, output containment, and receipt collisions
Inspected the emitted runtime report rather than relying on the command PASS line
Searched authority and design text for surviving skeleton, scaffold, mock, and NOT_APPLICABLE completion paths
COUNTERFACTUAL_CHECKS: The same observable validator rejects a success observation after the required stdout token is removed
The contract rejects a success scenario with no non-exit observable
The contract rejects a success scenario with no counterfactual
BOUNDARY_PROBES: Activation base rewrite is rejected against committed packet history
Runtime proof rewritten after activation is rejected by exact structural comparison
Shipping policy, lockfile, manifest, and toolchain changes become product-affecting once either base or current policy has a shipped member
Case-insensitive binary aliases, duplicate paths, unsafe segments, symlink inputs, and repository-contained stages are rejected
NEGATIVE_PATH_CHECKS: input collision with the staged binary
INDEPENDENT_FINDINGS: Closed: packet base SHA could be rewritten to hide product changes.
Closed: removing the last shipped-member declaration could disable packaging-impact classification.
Closed: staged inputs could overwrite the verified artifact or gate receipts.
Closed: the clean stage originally lived under build/target and therefore remained inside the repository.
Closed: symlink and path aliases could weaken fixture ownership or output containment.
Closed: governance-only reports originally overstated artifact and external-process proof classes.
RESIDUAL_UNCERTAINTY: No shipped Ferric member exists, so the production build-and-execute branch is compiled and structurally tested but cannot yet receive end-to-end runtime proof.

The gate executes outside the repository with stripped build variables but is not an operating-system security sandbox; code review and architecture checks still guard deliberate absolute filesystem or undeclared network access.

Declared production-boundary IDs cannot alone prove internal adapter selection; capability-specific observables, diagnostics, and protocol receipts must bind that claim in each future runtime scenario.
Timeout handling kills and reaps the direct child; descendant-process cleanup remains a required product-specific process-lifecycle proof when Ferric begins spawning external tools.
